company.name: Valneva SE
company.subtitle: Post-VALOR Phase 3 · IXCHIQ US withdrawn · S4V2 Phase 2 pending

assets.name: VLA15/LB6V (Lyme) — royalties
assets.partner: Pfizer (commercializes; Valneva receives 14-22% tiered royalties + up to $243M milestones)

market.notes: €150M = Valneva peak annual royalties assuming Pfizer does ~€800M in Lyme sales at ~19% blended royalty. penPct=100 means Valneva gets its full royalty; penMult scales Pfizer launch speed.

assets.name: IXCHIQ (Chikungunya)
assets.stage: US license suspended Aug 22, 2025; BLA+IND voluntarily withdrawn Jan 2026. Ex-US commercial (EU, Canada, UK, Brazil).

market.notes: US permanently lost. Ex-US includes EU/Canada/UK/Brazil + outbreak response. Competitor: Bavarian Nordic's Vimkunya (VLP-based, FDA approved Feb 2025). Ex-US contributed ~€7.5M in H1 2025.

assets.name: S4V2 (Shigella)
assets.stage: Phase 2 (two trials ongoing: infant safety/immunogenicity + Phase 2b CHIM human challenge)
assets.partner: LimmaTech Biologics (sponsors Phase 2; Valneva takes over if positive)

market.notes: WHO priority. No approved multivalent Shigella vaccine outside Russia/China. Global market estimated >$500M/yr. Second leading cause of fatal diarrhea worldwide.
Valneva will only advance if private/public funding materializes. Regulatory pathway and market opportunity remain uncertain.

IXIARO (Japanese encephalitis) + DUKORAL (cholera/ETEC). €157.9M total product sales 2025. 2026 guided €145-160M product sales. New CSL Seqirus distribution deal in Germany from Jan 2026. Third-party sales winding down to <5% by 2027.

bear.narrative: Lyme rejected + IXCHIQ dead + pipeline stalls
events: ✗ FDA rejects Lyme BLA — wide CI (15.8-93.5), requires additional efficacy trial
✗ IXCHIQ permanently withdrawn globally after further SAEs
✗ S4V2 Phase 2 CHIM study disappoints on efficacy
→ Travel vaccine company only, ~€155M rev, terminal multiple

base.narrative: Lyme approved with label nuance + IXCHIQ ex-US niche + S4V2 progresses
events: ✓ Pfizer files BLA Q3 2026 — FDA accepts despite wide CI
~ Lyme approved mid-2027 with post-market commitment or narrower label
~ IXCHIQ maintains ex-US sales (€15-25M/yr) via outbreak response
✓ S4V2 Phase 2 data positive mid-2026 → Valneva takes over development

bull.narrative: Lyme blockbuster + IXCHIQ ex-US growth + Shigella first-in-class
events: ✓ Lyme approved — first vaccine in 30 years, Pfizer launches globally 2028
✓ IXCHIQ ex-US reinstatement path or 2nd-gen candidate initiated
✓ S4V2 strong Phase 2 CHIM → fast-tracked Phase 3 as first-in-class
✓ Platform validated: 4th vaccine developed, partnership pipeline expanding

risks: Lyme CI too wide (15.8-93.5) — FDA may require additional efficacy trial before approval
IXCHIQ US permanently gone; ex-US at risk if further SAEs emerge globally
Lyme royalty stream depends entirely on Pfizer commercial execution + willingness to file
S4V2 still Phase 2 — no efficacy data yet; human challenge model may not predict field efficacy
Cash €109.7M (end 2025) — negative operating cash flow, €500M debt facility provides buffer
Travel vaccine market cyclical and exposed to geopolitical disruption
Pfizer could renegotiate Lyme terms or deprioritize launch
Bavarian Nordic's Vimkunya (VLP chikungunya) now competes for ex-US market

lastData.title: VALOR Phase 3 — Lyme vaccine VLA15 (with Pfizer)
points: 73.2% efficacy from 28 days post-dose 4 vs placebo (95% CI 15.8-93.5)
First prespecified analysis: stat criterion NOT met (CI lower bound <20% threshold)
Second prespecified analysis: MET statistical benchmark
Fewer Lyme cases than anticipated → study underpowered
Well tolerated, no safety concerns identified
Stock crashed 38% on the data (€4.56 → €2.78) despite positive efficacy
Pfizer 'confident in the vaccine's potential' and planning regulatory submissions

nextCatalyst.title: Pfizer files BLA (FDA) + MAA (EMA) for Lyme vaccine
thresholds: Pfizer confirmed planning to file in 2026 despite wide CI. BLA prep typically takes 4-6 months post-data. Filing acceptance is not guaranteed given statistical nuance.
Two trials: Phase 2 infant safety/immunogenicity + Phase 2b CHIM (human challenge model) sponsored by LimmaTech. If both positive → Valneva takes over all further development.
Standard 12-month BLA review. Could get Priority Review (6 months) given first-in-class + unmet need. This is THE value inflection. $143M regulatory milestones at stake.

components: Commercial (IXIARO + DUKORAL): steady-state revenue × EV/Revenue multiple. No discount rate — this is current recurring revenue.
Pipeline rNPV: risk-adjusted peak revenue × peak sales multiple, discounted at pipeline DR. Covers Lyme royalties, IXCHIQ ex-US, S4V2, Zika.
Pfizer milestones: up to €225M total, risk-adjusted by Lyme regulatory approval composite PoS.
methodology.formula: EV = (CommercialRev × CommercialMult) + (Σ PipelineRA × PipelineMult / (1+DR)^n) + (Milestones × LymeComposite) + Cash. TP = EV / 174M shares × (1-Dilution).
methodology.keyDebate: Lyme regulatory approval probability is the single biggest swing factor. 73% efficacy is clinically meaningful but the CI lower bound (15.8%) in the first analysis didn't meet the ≥20% threshold. The second analysis did meet it. Whether FDA accepts this is a genuine coin-flip debate.
methodology.note: The generic renderer needs updating to support SOTP (separate commercial and pipeline valuation). Until then, this JSON serves as the data source for the standalone valneva_model.html.
